programme.title: To the Manor Born: Britain's Best Loved Comedy

A look back at the hit sitcom starring Dame Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles.

programme.title: Lip-Reading the Royals: What Are They Really Saying?
programme.desc: Britain's most skilled lip readers look beyond the headlines to reveal what they believe was being said in the extraordinary days following the death of Queen Elizabeth II. As the nation absorbs the news, cameras capture a deeply personal exchange between Princess Anne and her husband. Our lip-readers believe their words reveal not protocol, but reflection. For the royal family, grief cannot pause duty. In one poignant moment before his first televised address, Charles is seen preparing himself for history. According to our experts, he appears to say: 'I certainly must address the nation, it's my duty. I will avoid the room until then'. It's a striking glimpse of the new King balancing private sorrow with public responsibility. Then there is the walkabout that made global headlines. William, Catherine, Harry and Meghan reunited to view the sea of flowers. The images suggest unity. Yet our lip-readers detected tension beneath the surface. This episode doesn't just follow history; it reveals the human side of the royals who are battling internal grief and public duty. Series Prod: Ben Algar; Exec Prod: Jonathan Levi; Dirs: Ben Algar/Jason Davidson/Pete Roch; Content Kings (S1 Ep3)
Forensic lip readers try to decode and reveal the royals' most guarded moments.

programme.title: Queen Victoria & the Groomsman: The Scandal That Nearly Brought Down the Empire
programme.desc: Exploring a passionate love affair that nearly changed the course of British history. This programme unravels one of the most provocative royal mysteries of the 19th century - a whispered romance between the widowed Queen Victoria and her devoted Highland servant John Brown. With newly unearthed evidence and expert analysis, this shocking documentary explores claims of a secret marriage, hidden correspondence, and a potential heir that could have rocked the foundations of the British monarchy. Blending historical intrigue with emotional depth, the programme invites viewers to reconsider the private passions behind the public persona of Britain's longest-reigning queen. Series Prod: Graeme Hart; Exec Prod: Pauline Law; IWC Media
